About.title: Mathias Mailly de pinho
About.description:
I am a two-year higher education student in web development, specializing in the MERN stack. 
 I am passionate about creating modern, high-performance, and intuitive web applications.

1.description: Project – Dynamic JavaScript Web Page. Development of a dynamic front-end application for an interior designer's portfolio, as part of a project simulating a web agency. This project implements: DOM manipulation via JavaScript, user event handling, data retrieval and processing via an API, and HTML/CSS and JavaScript integration. It demonstrates my ability to design interactive interfaces, structure clean front-end code, and adhere to specifications within a professional environment.

2.description: Kasa Real Estate Rental Web Application. Development of a modern apartment rental web application using React, as part of a web development course. This project simulates the Kasa platform, the leading French peer-to-peer rental platform with over 500 listings daily. Technologies used: React/React Router/SCSS/HTML/CSS. Key achievements: Initialization and configuration of a React application with Create React App, implementation of a fluid navigation system with React Router, development of reusable and optimized React components, creation of CSS animations for a dynamic user experience and a responsive interface adapted to all screen sizes. Result: A functional application with intuitive navigation, a modern design, and maintainable code adhering to React best practices.

3.description: REST API - Mon Vieux Grimoire. Back-end development of a book review website for a chain of bookstores in Old Lille, as part of a web development training course. Creation of a secure RESTful API allowing users to rate and manage literary works. Technologies: Node.js/Express/MongoDB/JavaScript. Main achievements: Design and implementation of a data model compliant with specifications. Development of complete and secure CRUD operations. Implementation of a secure data storage system. Management of user authentication and authorization. Image optimization (WebP format, compression). Adherence to Green Coding best practices for more sustainable code. Result: Functional, secure, and high-performance API with complete management of books, ratings, and users. Code available on GitHub and fully documented.

4.description: Project – Responsive homepage for a travel agency. Development of the homepage for a travel agency using HTML and CSS, based on Figma mockups designed for mobile, tablet, and desktop versions, within a context simulating a front-end development mission. This project involves: the accurate integration of a Figma mockup while respecting the provided design, the creation of a responsive interface adapted to all screen sizes, semantic structuring in HTML5, layout design and styling with CSS3, code validation according to W3C standards, and the use of a professional development environment with Visual Studio Code.

5.description: Project completed for a painter – Showcase website presenting their artworks. I had the opportunity to design and develop a showcase website dedicated to a painter, with the objective of highlighting their different artistic works through a modern, clear, and intuitive interface. The purpose of this project was to create a professional online presence to display the painter’s artworks, present their artistic universe, and make it easier for visitors to discover their work. This project allowed me to strengthen my front-end development skills, particularly in React, component management, responsive integration, and advanced styling structure using SCSS, while working on a concrete project focused on client needs and user experience.
